item.title: Why China Is Tightening Controls on Overseas Stock Trading
item.description: China’s efforts to control capital outflows are colliding with growing demand from mainland investors for access to overseas stocks.

item.title: MUFG Is Said to Explore Options for Stake in Indonesia’s Danamon
item.description: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. is exploring options for PT Bank Danamon Indonesia, including a take-private of the lender or selling some its stake to increase the public float, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: India Earnings Revival Under Threat as Commodity Prices Surge
item.description: Even if the Middle East war ends soon, corporate profits would stay under pressure, BofA says.

item.title: Wheat Extends Decline as Traders Digest Iran Ceasefire Talks
item.description: Wheat futures were on track to extend their longest losing streak since January, as traders returning from a US holiday digested progress in negotiations to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Sri Lanka Delivers Full-Point Rate Hike to Crimp Inflation
item.description: Sri Lanka’s central bank raised its policy rate to support the currency and contain inflationary pressures amid growing strain on the economy from the Iran war.

item.title: Chinese Chip Stocks Rise in Hong Kong on Hopes for Huawei Tech
item.description: Chinese semiconductor stocks rose in Hong Kong on optimism over a potential breakthrough in technology by Huawei Technologies Co.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Says Will Use Reserve Funds for Subsidies
item.description: The government will use about $3.2 billion from the reserve fund for this year’s annual budget to finance utility subsidies, Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

item.title: India’s Scorching Nights Strain Power Grid and Threaten Health
item.description: India’s dangerously torrid summer days have long been a climate warning. Now the country is increasingly dealing with sweltering nights too, as global warming and poor urban planning put millions at risk.

item.title: ASX Ramps Up Capital Spending to Support Technology Upgrade
item.description: ASX Ltd. is increasing its capital spending to upgrade critical market infrastructure, as Australia’s embattled exchange operator seeks to regain the confidence of regulators and stakeholders after years of technical issues.

item.title: Taiwan Overtakes India as World's Fifth-Largest Stock Market
item.description: Taiwan overtook India in stock market value, powered mainly by a breakneck rally in the world’s largest chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

item.title: Treasuries Rally as Trump Signals Progress in Iran Negotiations
item.description: Treasuries rallied across the curve as cash trading resumed after a holiday break, with investors growing more optimistic about a potential US-Iran deal.

item.title: Xiaomi Short Bets Hit Record on Cost Worries Ahead of Earnings
item.description: Traders pushed bearish bets on Xiaomi Corp. to a record ahead of first-quarter earnings amid concerns over rising memory costs and escalating rivalry in China’s electric vehicle market.

item.title: Japan Slips to World’s No. 3 Creditor Behind Germany and China
item.description: Japan was overtaken by China as the world’s second largest creditor nation in 2025, despite setting a fresh record for its tally of overseas assets.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as Iran Deal Progress Eases Inflation Concerns
item.description: Gold held gains on signs the US and Iran were making progress on reaching a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows, easing inflation fears.

item.title: These AI Gurus Are Charging Wall Street Banks $25,000 a Day
item.description: Global banks are pouring billions into artificial intelligence yet struggling to automate workflows. Two ex-bankers say they are being hired to ignite the shift.

item.title: A Scorching Asian Summer Will Add to Risk of Surging Gas Prices
item.description: As the global gas market grapples with the Strait of Hormuz being all-but closed for nearly three months, traders are fixated on two wildcards: China and the weather.

item.title: Japan Cablemaker Rout Exposes Cracks in AI Infrastructure Rally
item.description: A $40 billion selloff in a 141-year-old Japanese cable firm has served as a reality check on the fragility of the global AI-driven stock rally.

item.title: Treasury Curve Flashes Higher-for-Longer Warning Under Warsh
item.description: A key Treasury yield gap has shrunk to its tightest level in a year as traders ramp up bets the Federal Reserve may keep interest rates higher for longer under new chairman Kevin Warsh.

item.title: Codelco Union Threatens Protests as Misreporting Fallout Spreads
item.description: One of Codelco’s biggest unions vowed to fight any attempt to claw back bonuses as fallout from the overstatement of copper output at the Chilean state miner continues to spread.

item.title: Korea Short-Term Debt Pricing Too Many BOK Hikes, NH Amundi Says
item.description: South Korea’s bond market is pricing in an excessive number of central bank rate hikes, creating buying opportunities in short-term debt, according to NH Amundi Asset Management Co.

item.title: The Summer We Went Back to the Movies
item.description: For the first time since the pandemic shuttered cinemas the world over, Hollywood has got its mojo back.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Extend Gains on Iran Optimism: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were poised to extend gains as optimism over a potential US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz lifted risk appetite and sent oil prices lower.

item.title: Aegea Investors, Equatorial Bid in One of Brazil’s Biggest Deals This Year
item.description: Two groups submitted bids to become the strategic investor in Copasa, advancing the Minas Gerais state government’s plan to privatize one of Brazil’s largest water and sewage utilities, according to people familiar to the matter.

item.title: Oil Holds Decline as Traders See Progress Toward Iranian Deal
item.description: Oil held a drop on signs that negotiations to extend a US-Iranian ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz were making progress.

item.title: New Zealand’s Central Bank Set to Hold Rates and Stay in ‘Wait and See’ Mode
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank is expected to keep its key interest rate unchanged on Wednesday, opting to look through the inflationary impact of the Middle East energy shock in favor of supporting a fragile economic recovery.

item.title: Hungary Sees May 28 Deal With EU on Frozen Funds, PM Magyar Says
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he’ll sign a political accord with the head of the European Union’s executive on May 28 on the release of the bloc’s frozen funds.

item.title: Vigilant ECB Hasn’t Yet Seen Second-Round Effects, Villeroy Says
item.description: A spike in energy costs hasn’t yet turned into second-round effects in other parts of the euro-area economy, according to outgoing European Central Bank Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau.

item.title: Mexico to Host Iran Players for World Cup as War Tensions Simmer
item.description: Mexico will serve as the Iranian national team’s World Cup base after US authorities refused to let the players stay overnight on its soil amid ongoing military tensions.

item.title: Ghana to Start Buying 30% of Large Gold Mines’ Ouput From June
item.description: Ghana’s central bank said it plans to increase gold purchases from the West African nation’s large-scale producers to 30% of their output from 20%, starting June 1.

item.title: Cox Group Took 54% Bridge Loan for Deal to Buy Iberdrola Mexico
item.description: Spanish utility Cox ABG Group SA took a 54% bridge loan to help fund a $4.2 billion asset acquisition in Mexico and placed shares as collateral.
